title: Comparing Your Real Options for Payhip Alternatives for Restricted Countries — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan trying to sell ebooks, courses, or software, but PayPal, Stripe, and Payhip won't let you. You're stuck between trying to find a PayPal alternative for Nigeria digital sellers or figuring out how to sell digital products without a Stripe account. The truth is, these platforms block countries due to KYC requirements, banking limitations, or geo-restrictions, and you need a real solution to get paid.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayHip, Stripe, and PayPay are popular platforms for creators, but they have their limitations. PayHip offers a range of features, including digital product delivery and analytics, but it doesn't support all countries. Stripe is a payment processor, but it requires a business bank account and may not be available in your region. Meanwhile, Gumroad is another popular platform that doesn't support all countries either. But what about a dedicated solution for creators in restricted countries? That's where Unchained Commerce comes in, offering a range of products, including the Admin Dashboard, which provides transaction logs, order management, and real-time analytics, so you can see every order, every payment, every delivery — from anywhere in the world.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad block countries due to KYC requirements, banking limitations, or geo-restrictions. This leaves creators in restricted countries without a clear solution for selling digital products. But Unchained Commerce is different. It uses NOWPayments as its payment processor, which supports over 300 coins and doesn't require a KYC check or a buyer account. This means you can sell digital products without worrying about restrictions or limitations.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

The crypto payment alternative is not a workaround — it is a structurally different approach to commerce. NOWPayments operates as a crypto payment processor that requires no KYC from merchants and no account creation from buyers. It supports over 300 coins including Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDT on multiple chains, Solana, and Tron. Transactions settle directly to a wallet you control. There is no platform holding period, no payout schedule, and no compliance department deciding whether your country is permitted to receive funds. The Core Shop Engine integrates with NOWPayments via webhook, which means every confirmed transaction automatically triggers delivery — no manual steps, no delays, no dependency on a platform that can suspend your access.
PayPal's restrictions can block your sales, but Unchained Commerce has a solution.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.

So, which platform should you choose? If you're a creator in a restricted country, Unchained Commerce is the clear winner. Its range of products, including the Admin Dashboard, provides everything you need to get paid and sell digital products without any hassle. Plus, with NOWPayments as its payment processor, you can be sure that your payments will be processed quickly and securely.
